title: The Redesign Gap: Why 66% of Enterprises Are Using AI Without Changing How Work Gets Done
excerpt: 66% of enterprises use AI without redesigning a single process. Only 34% are truly reimagining workflows. Why the redesign gap is the most expensive invisible cost in enterprise AI.

title: The Supply Chain Gap: Why 200,000 AI Agent Instances Are Sitting on 150 Million Borrowed Trust
excerpt: 150M+ MCP SDK downloads, 200,000+ vulnerable instances, 40% of servers with zero authentication. The AI agent supply chain is the largest unsecured attack surface in enterprise history.

title: The Coordination Gap: Why 57% of Enterprises Deploy Multi-Agent Systems But Only 12% Coordinate Them Well
excerpt: 57% of enterprises have multi-agent systems in production but 40% fail within six months. Why coordination complexity is the new bottleneck in enterprise AI.

title: The Runtime Gap: Why 82% of Enterprise AI Never Reaches Production
excerpt: 82% of enterprise AI agents never reach production. 83% of those that do fail at scale due to infrastructure. Why the runtime — not the model — is the real bottleneck.

title: The Injection Gap: Why 85% of AI Coding Agents Execute Stranger Instructions
excerpt: 85% of AI coding agents follow injected instructions from fake error logs. 2,388 organizations exposed. Why prompt injection is the new software supply chain attack — and why the agent runtime is the only place to fix it.

title: The Identity Gap: Why 80% of AI Agents Are Running on Borrowed Credentials
excerpt: 80% of AI agents bypass IAM controls and only 21.9% have unique identities. The identity gap is the biggest security blindspot in enterprise AI.

title: The Sprawl Gap: Why 94% of Enterprises Fear AI Agent Proliferation — And Only 13% Are Ready for 150,000 Agents
excerpt: 94% of organizations fear AI agent sprawl. Gartner predicts Fortune 500 enterprises will go from 15 agents to 150,000+ by 2028. Why agent proliferation is IT's next crisis — and the six-step framework nobody's following.

title: The Evaluation Gap: Why 64% of Enterprises Can't Tell If Their AI Agents Are Actually Working
excerpt: 64% of leaders cite evaluation gaps as the top blocker for AI agent production. Only 31% of organizations have a measurement framework. Why most teams are flying blind on agent performance — and the three-layer framework that separates the 12% who ship from the 88% who don't.

title: The Data Debt: Why 85% of Leaders Say Data Gaps Are Killing Their AI Value
excerpt: 85% of leaders say enterprise debts limit AI value. Only 33% of enterprise data is AI-ready. Why the data foundation is the biggest unbudgeted liability in enterprise AI — and what the 6% of winners do differently.

title: The Fluency Gap: Why 79% of Enterprises Have the AI Budget but Not the AI Literacy to Use It
excerpt: 79% of organizations face AI adoption challenges despite record budgets. The problem isn't the technology — it's that nobody invested in AI literacy. A PM's take on the fluency gap.

title: The Value Chasm: Why 90% of Companies Are Doubling AI Budgets While Returns Stay Flat
excerpt: Nearly 40% of companies that measured AI cost savings landed below 10%. Yet 90% are increasing budgets again. The AI value gap is an organizational problem, not a technology problem.

title: The Deprecation Crisis: Why Model Version Churn Is the Hidden Cost Nobody Budgeted For
excerpt: Model versions are being deprecated faster than enterprises can re-deploy against them. Why version churn is quietly becoming your biggest unbudgeted AI cost.

title: The Observability Blindspot: Why 80% of Enterprises Can't Debug Their AI Agents
excerpt: Enterprises are shipping AI agents without the monitoring infrastructure to understand when they break. Why observability is the next bottleneck after governance.

title: The Mandate Trap: Why Performance Reviews Can't Force AI Adoption
excerpt: Meta made AI-driven impact a core performance criterion. Google is following. Why performance mandates won't close the AI adoption gap — and what actually will.

title: The Enforcement Gap: Why 82% of Execs Trust Their AI Policies But Only 14% Can Enforce Them
excerpt: 82% of executives trust their AI policies. Only 14% of organizations have enforcement at runtime. The 68-point enforcement gap is the real reason agent pilots fail.

title: The Great Cheapening: How 280x Cheaper Inference Rewrote the Economics of AI
excerpt: Inference costs dropped 280x from late 2022 to 2026. This single economic shift explains the AI adoption boom, the agentic turn, the governance crisis, and the $172B consumer windfall nobody is properly accounting for.

title: The 68-Point Gap: Why 88% of Companies Say They Use AI But Only 20% Actually Do
excerpt: 88% of organizations report using AI. The US Census Bureau says only 19.8% of businesses actually do. A PM's look at the biggest gap nobody's talking about.

title: The Governance Multiplier: Why AI Governance Pushes 12x More Projects to Production
excerpt: Companies with AI governance ship 12x more agentic projects to production. A PM's take on why governance is the hidden multiplier in enterprise AI.

title: The 88% Problem: Why Most AI Agent Pilots Never Reach Production
excerpt: 88% of AI agent pilots never reach production. A PM's perspective on why enterprise agent deployment stalls at governance, not model quality.

title: Software - what is it good for?
excerpt: Four fundamental uses of software: math, brokering human interactions, entertainment, and software for software.

title: GoodJira: Next Generation Project Management for AI Agents
excerpt: Exploring the need for project management tools for managing SWE-Agents

title: My bets in software
excerpt: Forecasting the future of software development and placing bets on how AI will transform the industry.
